NAME

main.c - 🖥️ Server main entry point: multi-client connection manager with per-client rendering threads (60fps video + 172fps audio)

int server_main (void)
Server mode entry point for unified binary.

mixer_t *volatile g_audio_mixer = NULL
Global audio mixer instance for multi-client audio processing. h265_server_context_t *volatile g_h265_server = NULL
Global H.265 codec server context. static_cond_t g_shutdown_cond = STATIC_COND_INIT
Global shutdown condition variable for waking blocked threads. rate_limiter_t * g_rate_limiter = NULL
Global rate limiter for connection attempts and packet processing. bool g_server_encryption_enabled = false
Global flag indicating if server encryption is enabled. private_key_t g_server_private_key = {0}
Global server private key (first identity key, for backward compatibility). private_key_t g_server_identity_keys [MAX_IDENTITY_KEYS] = {0}
Global server identity keys array (multi-key support). size_t g_num_server_identity_keys = 0
Number of loaded server identity keys. public_key_t g_client_whitelist [MAX_CLIENTS] = {0}
Global client public key whitelist. size_t g_num_whitelisted_clients = 0
Number of whitelisted clients.

Detailed Description

🖥️ Server main entry point: multi-client connection manager with per-client rendering threads (60fps video + 172fps audio)

MODULAR COMPONENTS:

  • main.c (this file): Server initialization, signal handling, connection management
  • client.c: Per-client lifecycle, threading, and state management
  • protocol.c: Network packet processing and protocol implementation
  • stream.c: Video mixing, ASCII frame generation, and caching
  • render.c: Per-client rendering threads with rate limiting
  • stats.c: Performance monitoring and resource tracking

CONCURRENCY MODEL:

The server creates multiple thread types per client:

1.
Receive thread: Handles incoming packets from client (protocol.c functions)
2.
Send thread: Manages outgoing packet delivery (client.c)
3.
Video render thread: Generates ASCII frames at 60fps (render.c)
4.
Audio render thread: Mixes audio streams at 172fps (render.c)
5.
Stats logger thread: Periodic performance reporting (stats.c)

Thread Safety:

  • Lock ordering: Always acquire g_client_manager_rwlock BEFORE per-client mutexes
  • Snapshot pattern: Copy client state under mutex, then process without locks
  • Signal-safe shutdown: SIGINT handler only sets flags and closes sockets
  • Deterministic cleanup: Main thread waits for all worker threads before exit

WHY THE REFACTORING:

The original server.c became unmaintainable at 2408+ lines, making it:

  • Too large for LLM context windows (limited AI-assisted development)
  • Difficult for humans to navigate and understand
  • Slow to compile and modify
  • Hard to isolate bugs and add new features

The modular design enables:

  • Faster development cycles (smaller compilation units)
  • Better IDE support (jump-to-definition, IntelliSense)
  • Easier testing and debugging (isolated components)
  • Future extensibility (new protocols, renderers, optimizations)

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

September 2025

Version

2.0 (Post-Modularization)

Definition in file server/main.c.

Function Documentation

int server_main (void )

Server mode entry point for unified binary. This function implements the complete server lifecycle including:

  • Server-specific initialization (crypto, shutdown callback)
  • Network socket setup and binding
  • Main connection accept loop
  • Client lifecycle management
  • Graceful shutdown and cleanup

Options are already parsed by the main dispatcher before this function is called, so they are available via global opt_* variables.

Returns

0 on success, non-zero error code on failure

Example

# Invoked by dispatcher after options are parsed:
ascii-chat server --port 8080
# Options parsed in main.c, then server_main() called

Definition at line 1784 of file server/main.c.

1785   // Common initialization (options, logging, lock debugging) now happens in main.c before dispatch
1786   // This function focuses on server-specific initialization

1812   }
1813   log_debug("Crypto initialized successfully");
1814 
1815   // Handle keepawake: check for mutual exclusivity and apply mode default

1820   if (GET_OPTION(enable_keepawake)) {

1824   log_info("ascii-chat server starting...");

1832 
1833   ascii_simd_init();
1834   precalc_rgb_palettes(weight_red, weight_green, weight_blue);
1835 
1836   // Simple signal handling (temporarily disable complex threading signal handling)
1837   log_debug("Setting up simple signal handlers...");
1838 
1839   // Handle Ctrl+C for cleanup
1840   platform_signal(SIGINT, server_handle_sigint);
1841   // Handle termination signal (SIGTERM is defined with limited support on Windows)
1842   platform_signal(SIGTERM, server_handle_sigterm);
1843 #ifndef _WIN32
1844   // SIGPIPE not supported on Windows
1845   platform_signal(SIGPIPE, SIG_IGN);
1846   // Note: SIGUSR1 is registered in src/main.c for all modes
1847 #endif
1848

1854   }

1857   // Create background worker thread pool for server operations

1863 
1864   // Spawn statistics logging thread in worker pool

1868     log_debug("Statistics logger thread started");
1869   }

1871   // Network setup - Use tcp_server abstraction for dual-stack IPv4/IPv6 binding
1872   log_debug("Config check: GET_OPTION(address)='%s', GET_OPTION(address6)='%s'", GET_OPTION(address),
1873             GET_OPTION(address6));
1874 
1875   bool ipv4_has_value = (strlen(GET_OPTION(address)) > 0);
1876   bool ipv6_has_value = (strlen(GET_OPTION(address6)) > 0);
1877   // Check if address is localhost (any loopback address, not just exact default)
1878   bool ipv4_is_default = is_localhost_ipv4(GET_OPTION(address));
1879   bool ipv6_is_default = is_localhost_ipv6(GET_OPTION(address6));
1880 
1881   log_debug("Binding decision: ipv4_has_value=%d, ipv6_has_value=%d, ipv4_is_default=%d, ipv6_is_default=%d",
1882             ipv4_has_value, ipv6_has_value, ipv4_is_default, ipv6_is_default);
1883 
1884   // Determine bind configuration
1885   bool bind_ipv4 = false;
1886   bool bind_ipv6 = false;
1887   const char *ipv4_address = NULL;
1888   const char *ipv6_address = NULL;
1889 
1890   if (ipv4_has_value && ipv6_has_value && ipv4_is_default && ipv6_is_default) {
1891     // Both are defaults: dual-stack with default localhost addresses
1892     bind_ipv4 = true;
1893     bind_ipv6 = true;
1894     ipv4_address = "127.0.0.1";
1895     ipv6_address = "::1";
1896     log_info("Default dual-stack: binding to 127.0.0.1 (IPv4) and ::1 (IPv6)");
1897   } else if (ipv4_has_value && !ipv4_is_default && (!ipv6_has_value || ipv6_is_default)) {
1898     // IPv4 explicitly set, IPv6 is default or empty: bind only IPv4
1899     bind_ipv4 = true;
1900     bind_ipv6 = false;
1901     ipv4_address = GET_OPTION(address);
1902     log_info("Binding only to IPv4 address: %s", ipv4_address);
1903   } else if (ipv6_has_value && !ipv6_is_default && (ipv4_is_default || !ipv4_has_value)) {
1904     // IPv6 explicitly set, IPv4 is default or empty: bind only IPv6
1905     bind_ipv4 = false;
1906     bind_ipv6 = true;
1907     ipv6_address = GET_OPTION(address6);
1908     log_info("Binding only to IPv6 address: %s", ipv6_address);
1909   } else {
1910     // Both explicitly set or one explicit + one default: dual-stack
1911     bind_ipv4 = true;
1912     bind_ipv6 = true;
1913     ipv4_address = ipv4_has_value ? GET_OPTION(address) : "127.0.0.1";
1914     ipv6_address = ipv6_has_value ? GET_OPTION(address6) : "::1";
1915     log_info("Dual-stack binding: IPv4=%s, IPv6=%s", ipv4_address, ipv6_address);
1916   }
1917

1933       .audio_mixer = g_audio_mixer,

1944   // Configure TCP server

1958   // Initialize TCP server (creates and binds sockets)
1959   memset(&g_tcp_server, 0, sizeof(g_tcp_server));
1960   asciichat_error_t tcp_init_result = tcp_server_init(&g_tcp_server, &tcp_config);
1961   if (tcp_init_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1962     // Signal shutdown to allow threads to exit before we call FATAL
1963     atomic_store_bool(&g_should_exit, true);
1964     log_debug("TCP server init failed, signaling shutdown to threads");
1965 
1966     // Clean up worker thread pool if it was created
1967     if (g_server_worker_pool) {
1968       log_debug("Early cleanup: destroying server worker pool");
1969       thread_pool_destroy(g_server_worker_pool);
1970       g_server_worker_pool = NULL;
1971     }
1972 
1973     FATAL(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize TCP server");
1974   }
1975

1999   // DEBUG: Print lock state immediately after WebSocket init

2001   // debug_sync_print_state();  // Disabled: causes AddressSanitizer stack-use-after-return crash

2003   // =========================================================================
2004   // UPnP Port Mapping (Quick Win for Direct TCP)
2005   // =========================================================================
2006   // Track UPnP success for ACDS session type decision
2007   // If UPnP fails, we need to create a WebRTC session to enable client connectivity
2008   bool upnp_succeeded = false;
2009 
2010   // Try to open port via UPnP so direct TCP works for ~70% of home users.
2011   // If this fails, clients fall back to WebRTC automatically - not fatal.
2012   //
2013   // Strategy:
2014   //   1. UPnP (works on ~90% of home routers)
2015   //   2. NAT-PMP fallback (Apple routers)
2016   //   3. If both fail: use ACDS + WebRTC (reliable, but slightly higher latency)
2017   if (GET_OPTION(enable_upnp)) {
2018     asciichat_error_t upnp_result = nat_upnp_open(port, "ascii-chat Server", &g_upnp_ctx);
2019 
2020     if (upnp_result == ASCIICHAT_OK && g_upnp_ctx) {
2021       char public_addr[22];
2022       if (nat_upnp_get_address(g_upnp_ctx, public_addr, sizeof(public_addr)) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2023         char msg[256];
2024         safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg), "🌐 Public endpoint: %s (direct TCP)", public_addr);
2025         log_console(LOG_INFO, msg);
2026         log_info("UPnP: Port mapping successful, public endpoint: %s", public_addr);
2027         upnp_succeeded = true;
2028       }
2029     } else {
2030       log_info("UPnP: Port mapping unavailable or failed - will use WebRTC fallback");
2031       log_console(LOG_INFO, "📡 Clients behind strict NATs will use WebRTC fallback");
2032     }
2033   } else {
2034     log_debug("UPnP: Disabled (use --upnp to enable automatic port mapping)");
2035   }
2036 
2037   // Initialize synchronization primitives
2038   if (rwlock_init(&g_client_manager_rwlock, "clients") != 0) {
2039     FATAL(ERROR_THREAD, "Failed to initialize client manager rwlock");
2040   }
2041 
2042   // Register global sync primitives for debugging
2043   NAMED_REGISTER_RWLOCK(&g_client_manager_rwlock, "client_manager_rwlock", NULL);
2044 
2045   // Lock debug system already initialized earlier in main()
2046 
2047   // Check if SIGINT/SIGTERM was received during initialization
2048   // If so, skip the accept loop entirely and go to cleanup
2049   if (atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2050     log_info("Shutdown signal received during initialization, skipping server startup");
2051     goto cleanup;
2052   }
2053 
2054   // Lock debug thread already started earlier in main()
2055 
2056   // NOTE: g_client_manager is already zero-initialized in client.c with = {0}
2057   // We only need to initialize the mutex
2058   mutex_init(&g_client_manager.mutex, "client_manager");
2059 
2060   // Initialize uthash head pointer for O(1) lookup (uthash requires NULL initialization)
2061   g_client_manager.clients_by_id = NULL;
2062 
2063   // Initialize connection rate limiter (prevents DoS attacks)
2064   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2065     log_debug("Initializing connection rate limiter...");
2066     g_rate_limiter = rate_limiter_create_memory();
2067     if (!g_rate_limiter) {
2068       LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to initialize rate limiter");
2069       if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2070         FATAL(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create connection rate limiter");
2071       }
2072     } else {
2073       log_info("Connection rate limiter initialized (50 connections/min per IP)");
2074     }
2075   }
2076 
2077   // Initialize audio mixer (always enabled on server)
2078   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2079     log_debug("Initializing audio mixer for per-client audio rendering...");
2080     g_audio_mixer = mixer_create(MAX_CLIENTS, AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE);
2081     if (!g_audio_mixer) {
2082       LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to initialize audio mixer");
2083       if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2084         FATAL(ERROR_AUDIO, "Failed to initialize audio mixer");
2085       }
2086     } else {
2087       if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2088         log_debug("Audio mixer initialized successfully for per-client audio rendering");
2089       }
2090     }
2091   }
2092 
2093   // Initialize H.265 codec server context (always enabled on server)
2094   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2095     log_debug("Initializing H.265 codec server context...");
2096     g_h265_server = h265_server_context_create();
2097     if (!g_h265_server) {
2098       LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to initialize H.265 codec context");
2099       if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2100         FATAL(ERROR_MEDIA_INIT, "Failed to initialize H.265 codec context");
2101       }
2102     } else {
2103       if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2104         log_debug("H.265 codec server context initialized successfully");
2105       }
2106     }
2107   }
2108 
2109   // Initialize mDNS context for LAN service discovery (optional)
2110   // mDNS allows clients on the LAN to discover this server without knowing its IP
2111   // Can be disabled with --no-mdns-advertise
2112   // Note: Actual advertisement is deferred until after ACDS session creation (if --acds is enabled)
2113   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit) && !GET_OPTION(no_mdns_advertise)) {
2114     log_debug("Initializing mDNS for LAN service discovery...");
2115     g_mdns_ctx = asciichat_mdns_init();
2116     if (!g_mdns_ctx) {
2117       LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to initialize mDNS (non-fatal, LAN discovery disabled)");
2118       log_warn("mDNS disabled - LAN service discovery will not be available");
2119       g_mdns_ctx = NULL;
2120     } else {
2121       log_debug("mDNS context initialized, advertisement deferred until session string is ready");
2122     }
2123   } else if (GET_OPTION(no_mdns_advertise)) {
2124     log_info("mDNS service advertisement disabled via --no-mdns-advertise");
2125   }
2126 
2127   // ========================================================================
2128   // Session Host Creation (for discovery mode support)
2129   // ========================================================================
2130   // Create session_host to track clients in a transport-agnostic way.
2131   // This enables future discovery mode where participants can become hosts.
2132   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2133     session_host_config_t host_config = {
2134         .port = port,
2135         .ipv4_address = ipv4_address,
2136         .ipv6_address = ipv6_address,
2137         .max_clients = GET_OPTION(max_clients),
2138         .encryption_enabled = g_server_encryption_enabled,
2139         .key_path = GET_OPTION(encrypt_key),
2140         .password = GET_OPTION(password),
2141         .callbacks = {0}, // No callbacks for now
2142         .user_data = NULL,
2143     };
2144 
2145     server_ctx.session_host = session_host_create(&host_config);
2146     if (!server_ctx.session_host) {
2147       // Non-fatal: session_host is optional, server can work without it
2148       log_warn("Failed to create session_host (discovery mode support disabled)");
2149     } else {
2150       log_debug("Session host created for discovery mode support");
2151     }
2152   }
2153 
2154   // ========================================================================
2155   // MAIN CONNECTION LOOP - Delegated to tcp_server
2156   // ========================================================================
2157   //
2158   // The tcp_server module handles:
2159   // 1. Dual-stack IPv4/IPv6 accept loop with select() timeout
2160   // 2. Spawning client_handler threads for each connection
2161   // 3. Responsive shutdown when g_tcp_server.running is set to false
2162   //
2163   // Client lifecycle is managed by ascii_chat_client_handler() which:
2164   // - Performs rate limiting
2165   // - Calls add_client() to initialize structures and spawn workers
2166   // - Blocks until client disconnects
2167   // - Calls remove_client() to cleanup and stop worker threads
2168 
2169   // ACDS Session Creation: Register this server with discovery service
2170   // This also determines the session string for mDNS (if --acds is enabled)
2171   char session_string[64] = {0};
2172   bool session_is_mdns_only = false;
2173 
2174   // ACDS Registration (conditional on --discovery flag)
2175   if (GET_OPTION(discovery)) {
2176     // Security Requirement Check (Issue #239):
2177     // Server IP must be protected by password, identity verification, or explicit opt-in
2178 
2179     // Auto-detection: Check if password or identity verification is configured
2180     const char *password = GET_OPTION(password);
2181     bool has_password = password && strlen(password) > 0;
2182     const char *encrypt_key = GET_OPTION(encrypt_key);
2183     bool has_identity = encrypt_key && strlen(encrypt_key) > 0;
2184     bool explicit_expose = GET_OPTION(discovery_expose_ip) != 0;
2185 
2186     // Validate security configuration BEFORE attempting ACDS connection
2187     bool acds_expose_ip_flag = false;
2188 
2189     if (has_password || has_identity) {
2190       // Auto-enable privacy: IP revealed only after verification
2191       acds_expose_ip_flag = false;
2192       log_plain("🔒 ACDS privacy enabled: IP disclosed only after %s verification",
2193                 has_password ? "password" : "identity");
2194     } else if (explicit_expose) {
2195       // Explicit opt-in to public IP disclosure
2196       // Only prompt if running interactively (can prompt user)
2197       // When non-interactive (automated/scripted), treat explicit flag as confirmation
2198       bool is_interactive = terminal_can_prompt_user();
2199 
2200       if (is_interactive) {
2201         log_warn("WARNING: You are about to allow PUBLIC IP disclosure!\n"
2202                  "Anyone with the session string will be able to see your IP address.\n"
2203                  "This is NOT RECOMMENDED unless you understand the privacy implications.");
2204 
2205         if (!platform_prompt_yes_no("Do you want to proceed with public IP disclosure", false)) {
2206           log_info("IP disclosure not confirmed. Server will run WITHOUT discovery service.");
2207           goto skip_acds_session;
2208         }
2209       }
2210 
2211       // User confirmed (or running non-interactively with explicit flag) - proceed with public IP disclosure
2212       acds_expose_ip_flag = true;
2213       log_info("Public IP disclosure CONFIRMED.\n"
2214                "Your IP address will be visible to anyone with the session string");
2215     } else {
2216       // Security violation: No password, no identity, no explicit opt-in
2217       log_error("Cannot create ACDS session: No security configured!\n"
2218                 "You must either:\n"
2219                 "1. Set a password: --password \"your-secret\"\n"
2220                 "2. Use identity key: --key ~/.ssh/id_ed25519\n"
2221                 "3. Explicitly allow public IP: --acds-expose-ip (NOT RECOMMENDED)\n"
2222                 "\n"
2223                 "Server will run WITHOUT discovery service.");
2224       goto skip_acds_session;
2225     }
2226 
2227     // Security is configured, proceed with ACDS connection
2228     const char *acds_server = GET_OPTION(discovery_server);
2229     uint16_t acds_port = (uint16_t)GET_OPTION(discovery_port);
2230 
2231     log_info("Attempting to create session on ACDS server at %s:%d...", acds_server, acds_port);
2232 
2233     acds_client_config_t acds_config;
2234     acds_client_config_init_defaults(&acds_config);
2235     SAFE_STRNCPY(acds_config.server_address, acds_server, sizeof(acds_config.server_address));
2236     acds_config.server_port = acds_port;
2237     acds_config.timeout_ms = 5 * MS_PER_SEC_INT;
2238 
2239     // Allocate ACDS client on heap for server lifecycle
2240     g_acds_client = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(acds_client_t), acds_client_t *);
2241     if (!g_acds_client) {
2242       log_error("Failed to allocate ACDS client");
2243       goto skip_acds_session;
2244     }
2245 
2246     asciichat_error_t acds_connect_result = acds_client_connect(g_acds_client, &acds_config);
2247     if (acds_connect_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2248       log_error("Failed to connect to ACDS server at %s:%d: %s", acds_server, acds_port,
2249                 asciichat_error_string(acds_connect_result));
2250       goto skip_acds_session;
2251     }
2252     if (acds_connect_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2253       // Prepare session creation parameters
2254       acds_session_create_params_t create_params;
2255       memset(&create_params, 0, sizeof(create_params));
2256 
2257       // Use server's Ed25519 identity public key if available
2258       if (g_server_encryption_enabled && has_identity) {
2259         memcpy(create_params.identity_pubkey, g_server_private_key.public_key, 32);
2260         log_debug("Using server identity key for ACDS session");
2261       } else {
2262         // No identity key available - use zero key
2263         // ACDS will accept this if identity verification is not required
2264         memset(create_params.identity_pubkey, 0, 32);
2265         log_debug("No server identity key - using zero key for ACDS session");
2266       }
2267 
2268       create_params.capabilities = 0x03; // Video + Audio
2269       create_params.max_participants = GET_OPTION(max_clients);
2270       log_debug("ACDS: max_clients option value = %d", GET_OPTION(max_clients));
2271 
2272       // Set password if configured
2273       create_params.has_password = has_password;
2274       if (has_password) {
2275         // TODO: Hash password with Argon2id
2276         SAFE_STRNCPY(create_params.password, password, sizeof(create_params.password));
2277       }
2278 
2279       // Set IP disclosure policy (determined above)
2280       create_params.acds_expose_ip = acds_expose_ip_flag;
2281       log_info("DEBUG: Server setting acds_expose_ip=%d (explicit_expose=%d, has_password=%d, has_identity=%d)",
2282                create_params.acds_expose_ip, explicit_expose, has_password, has_identity);
2283 
2284       // Set session type (Direct TCP or WebRTC)
2285       // Auto-detect: Use WebRTC if UPnP failed OR if explicitly requested via --webrtc
2286       // Exception: If bind address is 0.0.0.0, server is on public IP - use Direct TCP
2287       const char *bind_addr = GET_OPTION(address);
2288       bool bind_all_interfaces = (strcmp(bind_addr, "0.0.0.0") == 0);
2289 
2290       // Determine session type: prefer WebRTC by default (unless explicitly disabled)
2291       // Priority: explicit --webrtc flag > connection type detection > UPnP > default
2292       if (GET_OPTION(webrtc)) {
2293         // Explicit WebRTC request
2294         create_params.session_type = SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC;
2295         log_info("ACDS session type: WebRTC (explicitly requested via --webrtc)");
2296       } else if (bind_all_interfaces) {
2297         // Bind to 0.0.0.0: use WebRTC as default (better NAT compatibility)
2298         create_params.session_type = SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC;
2299         log_info("ACDS session type: WebRTC (default for 0.0.0.0 binding, provides NAT-agnostic connections)");
2300       } else if (upnp_succeeded) {
2301         // UPnP port mapping worked - can use direct TCP
2302         create_params.session_type = SESSION_TYPE_DIRECT_TCP;
2303         log_info("ACDS session type: Direct TCP (UPnP succeeded, server is publicly accessible)");
2304       } else {
2305         // UPnP failed and not on public IP - use WebRTC for NAT traversal
2306         create_params.session_type = SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC;
2307         log_info("ACDS session type: WebRTC (UPnP failed, server behind NAT)");
2308       }
2309 
2310       // Server connection information (where clients should connect)
2311       // If bind address is 0.0.0.0, leave server_address empty for ACDS to auto-detect public IP
2312       if (bind_all_interfaces) {
2313         create_params.server_address[0] = '\0'; // Empty - ACDS will use connection source IP
2314         log_debug("Bind address is 0.0.0.0, ACDS will auto-detect public IP from connection");
2315       } else {
2316         SAFE_STRNCPY(create_params.server_address, bind_addr, sizeof(create_params.server_address));
2317       }
2318       create_params.server_port = port;
2319 
2320       // DEBUG: Log what we're sending to ACDS
2321       log_info("DEBUG: Before SESSION_CREATE - expose_ip_publicly=%d, server_address='%s' port=%u, session_type=%u",
2322                create_params.acds_expose_ip, create_params.server_address, create_params.server_port,
2323                create_params.session_type);
2324 
2325       // Create session
2326       acds_session_create_result_t create_result;
2327       asciichat_error_t create_err = acds_session_create(g_acds_client, &create_params, &create_result);
2328 
2329       if (create_err == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2330         SAFE_STRNCPY(session_string, create_result.session_string, sizeof(session_string));
2331         SAFE_STRNCPY(g_session_string, create_result.session_string, sizeof(g_session_string));
2332         session_is_mdns_only = false; // Session is now registered with ACDS (globally discoverable)
2333         log_info("Session created: %s", session_string);
2334 
2335         // Server must join its own session so ACDS can route signaling messages
2336         log_debug("Server joining session as first participant for WebRTC signaling...");
2337         acds_session_join_params_t join_params = {0};
2338         join_params.session_string = session_string;
2339 
2340         // Use same identity key as session creation
2341         memcpy(join_params.identity_pubkey, create_params.identity_pubkey, 32);
2342 
2343         // Include password if session is password-protected
2344         if (has_password) {
2345           join_params.has_password = true;
2346           SAFE_STRNCPY(join_params.password, password, sizeof(join_params.password));
2347         }
2348 
2349         acds_session_join_result_t join_result = {0};
2350         asciichat_error_t join_err = acds_session_join(g_acds_client, &join_params, &join_result);
2351         if (join_err != ASCIICHAT_OK || !join_result.success) {
2352           log_error("Failed to join own session: %s (error: %s)", asciichat_error_string(join_err),
2353                     join_result.error_message[0] ? join_result.error_message : "unknown");
2354           // Continue anyway - this is not fatal for Direct TCP sessions
2355         } else {
2356           log_debug("Server joined session successfully (participant_id: %02x%02x...)", join_result.participant_id[0],
2357                     join_result.participant_id[1]);
2358           // Store participant ID for WebRTC signaling (needed to identify server in SDP/ICE messages)
2359           memcpy(g_server_participant_id, join_result.participant_id, 16);
2360           log_debug("Stored server participant_id for signaling: %02x%02x...", g_server_participant_id[0],
2361                     g_server_participant_id[1]);
2362           memcpy(create_result.session_id, join_result.session_id, 16);
2363         }
2364 
2365         // Keep ACDS connection alive for WebRTC signaling relay
2366         log_debug("Server staying connected to ACDS for signaling relay");
2367 
2368         // Create ACDS transport wrapper for sending signaling packets
2369         g_acds_transport = acip_tcp_transport_create("transport_acds_server", g_acds_client->socket, NULL);
2370         if (!g_acds_transport) {
2371           log_error("Failed to create ACDS transport wrapper");
2372         } else {
2373           log_debug("ACDS transport wrapper created for signaling");
2374 
2375           // Start ACDS ping thread to keep connection alive (for ALL session types)
2376           int ping_thread_result = asciichat_thread_create(&g_acds_ping_thread, "acds_ping", acds_ping_thread, NULL);
2377           if (ping_thread_result != 0) {
2378             log_error("Failed to create ACDS ping thread: %d", ping_thread_result);
2379           } else {
2380             log_debug("ACDS ping thread started to keep connection alive");
2381             g_acds_ping_thread_started = true;
2382           }
2383         }
2384 
2385         // Initialize WebRTC peer_manager if session type is WebRTC
2386         if (create_params.session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC) {
2387           log_debug("Initializing WebRTC library and peer manager for session (role=CREATOR)...");
2388 
2389           // Initialize WebRTC library (libdatachannel)
2390           asciichat_error_t webrtc_init_result = webrtc_init();
2391           if (webrtc_init_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2392             log_error("Failed to initialize WebRTC library: %s", asciichat_error_string(webrtc_init_result));
2393             g_webrtc_peer_manager = NULL;
2394           } else {
2395             log_debug("WebRTC library initialized successfully");
2396 
2397             // Configure STUN servers for ICE gathering (static to persist for peer_manager lifetime)
2398             static stun_server_t stun_servers[4] = {0};
2399             static lifecycle_t g_stun_lc = LIFECYCLE_INIT;
2400             static int stun_count = 0; // Store actual count separately
2401 
2402             if (lifecycle_init(&g_stun_lc, "stun")) {
2403               log_debug("Parsing STUN servers from options: '%s'", GET_OPTION(stun_servers));
2404               int count =
2405                   stun_servers_parse(GET_OPTION(stun_servers), OPT_ENDPOINT_STUN_SERVERS_DEFAULT, stun_servers, 4);
2406               if (count > 0) {
2407                 stun_count = count;
2408                 log_debug("Parsed %d STUN servers", count);
2409                 for (int i = 0; i < count; i++) {
2410                   log_debug("  STUN[%d]: '%s' (len=%d)", i, stun_servers[i].host, stun_servers[i].host_len);
2411                 }
2412               } else {
2413                 log_warn("Failed to parse STUN servers, using defaults");
2414                 stun_count = stun_servers_parse(OPT_ENDPOINT_STUN_SERVERS_DEFAULT, OPT_ENDPOINT_STUN_SERVERS_DEFAULT,
2415                                                 stun_servers, 4);
2416                 log_debug("Using default STUN servers, count=%d", stun_count);
2417                 for (int i = 0; i < stun_count; i++) {
2418                   log_debug("  STUN[%d]: '%s' (len=%d)", i, stun_servers[i].host, stun_servers[i].host_len);
2419                 }
2420               }
2421             }
2422 
2423             // Configure peer_manager
2424             webrtc_peer_manager_config_t pm_config = {
2425                 .role = WEBRTC_ROLE_CREATOR, // Server accepts offers, generates answers
2426                 .stun_servers = stun_servers,
2427                 .stun_count = stun_count,
2428                 .turn_servers = NULL, // No TURN for server (clients should have public IP or use TURN)
2429                 .turn_count = 0,
2430                 .on_transport_ready = on_webrtc_transport_ready,
2431                 .user_data = &server_ctx,
2432                 .crypto_ctx = NULL // WebRTC handles crypto internally
2433             };
2434 
2435             // Configure signaling callbacks for relaying SDP/ICE via ACDS
2436             webrtc_signaling_callbacks_t signaling_callbacks = {
2437                 .send_sdp = server_send_sdp, .send_ice = server_send_ice, .user_data = NULL};
2438 
2439             // Create peer_manager
2440             asciichat_error_t pm_result =
2441                 webrtc_peer_manager_create(&pm_config, &signaling_callbacks, &g_webrtc_peer_manager);
2442             if (pm_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2443               log_error("Failed to create WebRTC peer_manager: %s", asciichat_error_string(pm_result));
2444               g_webrtc_peer_manager = NULL;
2445             } else {
2446               log_debug("WebRTC peer_manager initialized successfully");
2447 
2448               // Start ACDS receive thread for WebRTC signaling relay
2449               int thread_result =
2450                   asciichat_thread_create(&g_acds_receive_thread, "acds_recv", acds_receive_thread, NULL);
2451               if (thread_result != 0) {
2452                 log_error("Failed to create ACDS receive thread: %d", thread_result);
2453                 // Cleanup peer_manager since signaling won't work
2454                 webrtc_peer_manager_destroy(g_webrtc_peer_manager);
2455                 g_webrtc_peer_manager = NULL;
2456               } else {
2457                 log_debug("ACDS receive thread started for WebRTC signaling relay");
2458                 g_acds_receive_thread_started = true;
2459               }
2460             }
2461           } // Close else block from webrtc_init() success
2462         } else {
2463           log_debug("Session type is DIRECT_TCP, skipping WebRTC peer_manager initialization");
2464         }
2465 
2466         // Advertise mDNS with ACDS session string
2467         // This ensures both mDNS and ACDS discovery return the same session string
2468         advertise_mdns_with_session(session_string, (uint16_t)port);
2469       } else {
2470         log_warn("Failed to create session on ACDS server (server will run without discovery)");
2471         // Clean up failed ACDS client
2472         if (g_acds_client) {
2473           acds_client_disconnect(g_acds_client);
2474           SAFE_FREE(g_acds_client);
2475           g_acds_client = NULL;
2476         }
2477       }
2478     } else {
2479       log_warn("Could not connect to ACDS server at %s:%d (server will run without discovery)", acds_server, acds_port);
2480     }
2481   } else {
2482     log_info("ACDS registration disabled (use --acds to enable)");
2483   }
2484 
2485 skip_acds_session:
2486   // Fallback: If no session string was set by ACDS (either disabled or failed),
2487   // generate a random session string for mDNS discovery only
2488   if (session_string[0] == '\0' && g_mdns_ctx) {
2489     log_debug("No ACDS session string available, generating random session for mDNS");
2490 
2491     // Use the proper session string generation from discovery module
2492     // This generates adjective-noun-noun format using the full wordlists
2493     if (acds_string_generate(session_string, sizeof(session_string)) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2494       log_error("Failed to generate session string for mDNS");
2495       return 1;
2496     }
2497 
2498     log_debug("Generated random session string for mDNS: '%s'", session_string);
2499 
2500     // Mark that this session is mDNS-only (not globally discoverable via ACDS)
2501     session_is_mdns_only = true;
2502 
2503     // Advertise mDNS with random session string
2504     advertise_mdns_with_session(session_string, (uint16_t)port);
2505   }
2506 
2507   // ====================================================================
2508   // Display session string prominently as the FINAL startup message
2509   // This ensures users see the connection info clearly without logs
2510   // wiping it away
2511   // ====================================================================
2512   if (session_string[0] != '\0') {
2513     if (session_is_mdns_only) {
2514       log_plain("━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━\n📋 Session String: %s (LAN only via "
2515                 "mDNS)\n🔗 Share with others on your LAN to join:\n   ascii-chat "
2516                 "%s\n━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━",
2517                 session_string, session_string);
2518     } else {
2519       log_plain("━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━\n📋 Session String: %s\n🔗 Share this "
2520                 "globally to join:\n   ascii-chat %s\n━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━",
2521                 session_string, session_string);
2522     }
2523   }
2524 
2525   // Copy session info to globals for status screen display
2526   SAFE_STRNCPY(g_session_string, session_string, sizeof(g_session_string));
2527   g_session_is_mdns_only = session_is_mdns_only;
2528 
2529   log_debug("Server entering accept loop (port %d)...", port);
2530 
2531   // Initialize status screen
2532   g_server_start_time = time(NULL);
2533   g_last_status_update = platform_get_monotonic_time_us();
2534 
2535   // Clear status screen log buffer to discard initialization logs
2536   // This ensures only NEW logs (generated after status screen starts) are displayed
2537   if (GET_OPTION(status_screen)) {
2538     ui_status_log_clear();
2539   }
2540 
2541   // Start status screen thread if enabled
2542   // Runs independently at target FPS (default 60 Hz), decoupled from network accept loop
2543   if (GET_OPTION(status_screen)) {
2544     if (asciichat_thread_create(&g_status_screen_thread, "status_screen", status_screen_thread, NULL) != 0) {
2545       log_error("Failed to create status screen thread");
2546       goto cleanup;
2547     }
2548     log_debug("Status screen thread started");
2549   }
2550 
2551   // Start WebSocket server thread if initialized
2552   if (g_websocket_server.context != NULL) {
2553     if (asciichat_thread_create(&g_websocket_server_thread, "ws_server", websocket_server_thread_wrapper,
2554                                 &g_websocket_server) != 0) {
2555       log_error("Failed to create WebSocket server thread");
2556     } else {
2557       g_websocket_server_thread_started = true;
2558       log_info("WebSocket server thread started");
2559     }
2560   }
2561 
2562   // Run TCP server (blocks until shutdown signal received)
2563   // tcp_server_run() handles:
2564   // - select() on IPv4/IPv6 sockets with timeout
2565   // - accept() new connections
2566   // - Spawn ascii_chat_client_handler() thread for each connection
2567   // - Responsive shutdown when atomic_store_bool(&g_tcp_server.running, false)
2568   asciichat_error_t run_result = tcp_server_run(&g_tcp_server);
2569   if (run_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2570     log_error("TCP server exited with error");
2571   }
2572 
2573   log_debug("Server accept loop exited");
2574 
2575 cleanup:
2576   // Signal status screen thread to exit
2577   atomic_store_bool(&g_should_exit, true);
2578 
2579   // Wait for status screen thread to finish if it was started
2580   if (GET_OPTION(status_screen)) {
2581     log_debug("Waiting for status screen thread to exit...");
2582     int join_result = asciichat_thread_join_timeout(&g_status_screen_thread, NULL, NS_PER_SEC_INT); // 1 second
2583     if (join_result != 0) {
2584       log_warn("Status screen thread did not exit cleanly (timeout)");
2585     } else {
2586       log_debug("Status screen thread exited");
2587     }
2588   }
2589 
2590   // Cleanup
2591   log_debug("Server shutting down...");
2592   memset(g_session_string, 0, sizeof(g_session_string)); // Clear session string for status screen
2593 
2594   // Wake up any threads that might be blocked on condition variables
2595   // (like packet queues) to ensure responsive shutdown
2596   // This must happen BEFORE client cleanup to wake up any blocked threads
2597   static_cond_broadcast(&g_shutdown_cond);
2598   // NOTE: Do NOT call cond_destroy() on statically-initialized condition variables
2599   // g_shutdown_cond uses STATIC_COND_INIT which doesn't allocate resources that need cleanup
2600   // Calling cond_destroy() on a static cond is undefined behavior on some platforms
2601 
2602   // Close all client sockets immediately to unblock receive threads.
2603   // The signal handler only closed the listening socket, but client receive threads
2604   // are still blocked in recv_with_timeout(). We need to close their sockets to unblock them.
2605   log_debug("Closing all client sockets to unblock receive threads...");
2606 
2607   // Use write lock since we're modifying client->socket
2608   rwlock_wrlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2609   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
2610     client_info_t *client = &g_client_manager.clients[i];
2611     if (client->client_id[0] != '\0' && client->socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
2612       socket_close(client->socket);
2613       client->socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
2614     }
2615   }
2616   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2617 
2618   log_debug("Signaling all clients to stop (sockets closed, g_should_exit set)...");
2619 
2620   // Stop and destroy server worker thread pool (stats logger, etc.)
2621   if (g_server_worker_pool) {
2622     thread_pool_destroy(g_server_worker_pool);
2623     g_server_worker_pool = NULL;
2624     log_debug("Server worker thread pool stopped");
2625   }
2626 
2627   // Destroy rate limiter
2628   if (g_rate_limiter) {
2629     rate_limiter_destroy(g_rate_limiter);
2630     g_rate_limiter = NULL;
2631   }
2632 
2633   // Shutdown WebSocket server BEFORE removing clients.
2634   // The LWS event thread runs callbacks that access transport data (impl_data).
2635   // If we destroy transports via remove_client while LWS is still running,
2636   // callbacks access freed memory (heap-use-after-free).
2637   // lws_context_destroy fires LWS_CALLBACK_CLOSED for all connections, which
2638   // closes transports and NULLs the per-session conn_data->transport pointer.
2639   // After this, remove_client can safely destroy the transport objects.
2640   bool websocket_exited_cleanly = true;  // Default to clean unless timeout detected
2641   if (g_websocket_server.context != NULL) {
2642     log_debug("Shutting down WebSocket server before client cleanup");
2643     atomic_store_u64(&g_websocket_server.running, false);
2644     // Wake the LWS event loop immediately instead of waiting for the 50ms
2645     // lws_service timeout. This is safe because the event loop thread can't
2646     // destroy the context until after it exits its loop (which requires
2647     // seeing running=false, which we just set).
2648     websocket_server_cancel_service(&g_websocket_server);
2649 
2650     // Join with 500ms timeout. If the WebSocket thread is deadlocked in lws_service(),
2651     // it won't exit and we skip the graceful handler thread cleanup to avoid prolonged
2652     // shutdown hangs. Forceful cleanup will happen in websocket_server_destroy().
2653     if (g_websocket_server_thread_started) {
2654       int join_result =
2655           asciichat_thread_join_timeout(&g_websocket_server_thread, NULL, 500 * NS_PER_MS_INT); // 500ms in ns
2656       if (join_result != 0) {
2657         log_warn("WebSocket thread did not exit within 500ms, skipping graceful handler cleanup");
2658         websocket_exited_cleanly = false;
2659       }
2660       g_websocket_server_thread_started = false;
2661     }
2662 
2663     if (websocket_exited_cleanly) {
2664       log_debug("WebSocket thread confirmed exited, waiting for handler threads to complete...");
2665     }
2666   }
2667 
2668   // Wait for all client handler threads to exit BEFORE destroying WebSocket context
2669   // Handler threads may still be working with WebSocket connections, so we must
2670   // ensure they complete before destroying the context (which would abort their operations).
2671   // Skip this if WebSocket thread didn't exit cleanly (deadlock detected).
2672   if (websocket_exited_cleanly) {
2673     int retry_count = 0;
2674     while (retry_count < 50) { // Max 500ms wait
2675       rwlock_rdlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2676       bool all_cleaned = true;
2677       for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
2678         if (g_client_manager.clients[i].client_id[0] != '\0') {
2679           all_cleaned = false;
2680           break;
2681         }
2682       }
2683       rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2684 
2685       if (all_cleaned)
2686         break;
2687       platform_sleep_us(10 * US_PER_MS_INT); // 10ms
2688       retry_count++;
2689     }
2690     log_debug("Client handler threads cleaned up, destroying WebSocket server");
2691   } else {
2692     log_warn("Skipping graceful handler thread cleanup due to WebSocket deadlock");
2693   }
2694 
2695   // ★ CRITICAL: Cleanup debug sync BEFORE destroying websocket_server
2696   // The debug_sync thread runs concurrently and accesses the named registry.
2697   // If websocket_server_destroy() frees thread pool memory first, the debug thread
2698   // will get a use-after-free error trying to check condition variables.
2699   // Moving this before websocket destroy prevents the race condition.
2700 #ifndef NDEBUG
2701   debug_sync_destroy();
2702 #endif
2703 
2704   // Now destroy WebSocket server after all handler threads have completed
2705   // This ensures no handler threads are still accessing the context.
2706   // (It's safe to call even if context was already destroyed forcefully above)
2707   websocket_server_destroy(&g_websocket_server);
2708   log_debug("WebSocket server destroyed");
2709 
2710   // Cleanup status screen log capture (must be AFTER all client threads exit to avoid use-after-free)
2711   // Client threads may still be appending logs during the wait loop above, so we destroy the buffer only
2712   // after we've confirmed they've all exited.
2713   ui_status_log_destroy();
2714 
2715   // Clean up all connected clients
2716   log_debug("Cleaning up connected clients...");
2717   // FIXED: Simplified to collect client IDs first, then remove them without holding locks
2718   char clients_to_remove[MAX_CLIENTS][MAX_CLIENT_ID_LEN];
2719   int client_count = 0;
2720 
2721   rwlock_rdlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2722   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
2723     client_info_t *client = &g_client_manager.clients[i];
2724 
2725     // Only attempt to clean up clients that were actually connected
2726     // (client_id is empty string for uninitialized clients)
2727     // FIXED: Only access mutex for initialized clients to avoid accessing uninitialized mutex
2728     if (client->client_id[0] == '\0') {
2729       continue; // Skip uninitialized clients
2730     }
2731 
2732     // Use snapshot pattern to avoid holding both locks simultaneously
2733     // This prevents deadlock by not acquiring client_state_mutex while holding rwlock
2734     SAFE_STRNCPY(clients_to_remove[client_count], client->client_id, MAX_CLIENT_ID_LEN - 1);
2735 
2736     // Clean up ANY client that was allocated, whether active or not
2737     // (disconnected clients may not be active but still have resources)
2738     client_count++;
2739   }
2740   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2741 
2742   // Remove all clients without holding any locks
2743   for (int i = 0; i < client_count; i++) {
2744     if (remove_client(&server_ctx, clients_to_remove[i]) != 0) {
2745       log_error("Failed to remove client %s during shutdown", clients_to_remove[i]);
2746     }
2747   }
2748 
2749   // Clean up hash table
2750   // Cannot use HASH_ITER when deleting - HASH_DELETE modifies the table and can set
2751   // the pointer to NULL, causing HASH_ITER to dereference a null pointer.
2752   // Instead, repeatedly delete the first item until the table is empty.
2753   while (g_client_manager.clients_by_id) {
2754     client_info_t *current_client = g_client_manager.clients_by_id;
2755     HASH_DELETE(hh, g_client_manager.clients_by_id, current_client);
2756     // Note: We don't free current_client here because it's part of the clients[] array
2757   }
2758 
2759   // Clean up audio mixer
2760   if (g_audio_mixer) {
2761     // Set to NULL first before destroying.
2762     // Client handler threads may still be running and checking g_audio_mixer.
2763     // Setting it to NULL first prevents use-after-free race condition.
2764     // volatile ensures this write is visible to other threads immediately
2765     mixer_t *mixer_to_destroy = g_audio_mixer;
2766     g_audio_mixer = NULL;
2767     mixer_destroy(mixer_to_destroy);
2768   }
2769 
2770   // Clean up H.265 codec server context
2771   if (g_h265_server) {
2772     // Set to NULL first before destroying, similar to g_audio_mixer
2773     // Client handler threads may still be running and checking g_h265_server.
2774     h265_server_context_t *h265_to_destroy = g_h265_server;
2775     g_h265_server = NULL;
2776     h265_server_context_destroy(h265_to_destroy);
2777     log_debug("H.265 codec server context shut down");
2778   }
2779 
2780   // Clean up mDNS context
2781   if (g_mdns_ctx) {
2782     asciichat_mdns_destroy(g_mdns_ctx);
2783     g_mdns_ctx = NULL;
2784     log_debug("mDNS context shut down");
2785   }
2786 
2787   // Clean up synchronization primitives
2788   rwlock_destroy(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2789   mutex_destroy(&g_client_manager.mutex);
2790 
2791 #ifndef NDEBUG
2792   // Clean up statistics system
2793   stats_cleanup();
2794 
2795   // Note: debug_sync_destroy() is now called earlier (before websocket_server_destroy)
2796   // to avoid use-after-free race condition with the debug monitoring thread
2797 #endif
2798 
2799   // Destroy session host (before TCP server shutdown)
2800   if (server_ctx.session_host) {
2801     log_debug("Destroying session host");
2802     session_host_destroy(server_ctx.session_host);
2803     server_ctx.session_host = NULL;
2804   }
2805 
2806   // Destroy H.265 server codec context
2807   if (server_ctx.h265_server) {
2808     log_debug("Destroying H.265 server context");
2809     h265_server_context_destroy(server_ctx.h265_server);
2810     server_ctx.h265_server = NULL;
2811   }
2812 
2813   // Shutdown TCP server (closes listen sockets and cleans up)
2814   tcp_server_destroy(&g_tcp_server);
2815 
2816   // WebSocket server already shut down before client cleanup (above).
2817 
2818   // Join ACDS threads (if started)
2819   // NOTE: Must be done BEFORE destroying transport to ensure clean shutdown
2820   if (g_acds_ping_thread_started) {
2821     log_debug("Joining ACDS ping thread");
2822     asciichat_thread_join(&g_acds_ping_thread, NULL);
2823     g_acds_ping_thread_started = false;
2824     log_debug("ACDS ping thread joined");
2825   }
2826 
2827   if (g_acds_receive_thread_started) {
2828     log_debug("Joining ACDS receive thread");
2829     asciichat_thread_join(&g_acds_receive_thread, NULL);
2830     g_acds_receive_thread_started = false;
2831     log_debug("ACDS receive thread joined");
2832   }
2833 
2834   // Clean up WebRTC peer manager (if initialized for ACDS signaling relay)
2835   if (g_webrtc_peer_manager) {
2836     log_debug("Destroying WebRTC peer manager");
2837     webrtc_peer_manager_destroy(g_webrtc_peer_manager);
2838     g_webrtc_peer_manager = NULL;
2839   }
2840 
2841   // Clean up ACDS transport wrapper (if created)
2842   if (g_acds_transport) {
2843     log_debug("Destroying ACDS transport wrapper");
2844     acip_transport_destroy(g_acds_transport);
2845     g_acds_transport = NULL;
2846   }
2847 
2848   // Disconnect from ACDS server (if connected for WebRTC signaling relay)
2849   if (g_acds_client) {
2850     log_debug("Disconnecting from ACDS server");
2851     acds_client_disconnect(g_acds_client);
2852     SAFE_FREE(g_acds_client);
2853     g_acds_client = NULL;
2854   }
2855   // Disable keepawake mode (re-allow OS to sleep)
2856   platform_disable_keepawake();
2857 
2858   // Clean up platform-specific resources (Windows: Winsock cleanup, timer restoration)
2859   // POSIX: minimal cleanup (symbol cache already handled above on Windows)
2860   socket_cleanup();
2861 
2862   log_info("Server shutdown complete");
2863 
2864   asciichat_error_stats_print();
2865 
2866   // Use exit() to allow atexit() handlers to run
2867   // Cleanup functions are idempotent (check if initialized first)
2868   return 0;
2869 }

Variable
  Documentation




mixer_t* volatile g_audio_mixer = NULL


Global audio mixer instance for multi-client audio processing.
    Global audio mixer.




The mixer combines audio streams from multiple clients, excluding
    each client's own audio from their outbound stream (preventing echo).
    Created once during server initialization and shared by all audio render
    threads.




THREAD SAFETY: The mixer itself is thread-safe and can be used
    concurrently by multiple render.c audio threads without external
    synchronization. During shutdown, set to NULL before destroying to prevent
    use-after-free.


Definition at line 174 of file server/main.c.


Referenced by add_client(), add_webrtc_client(),
    client_audio_render_thread(), remove_client(), and
    server_main().






public_key_t g_client_whitelist[MAX_CLIENTS] =
  {0}


Global client public key whitelist. Whitelist of allowed client
    public keys.




Array of public keys for clients that are authorized to connect to
    the server. Used for client authentication when whitelist mode is enabled.
    Sized to hold up to MAX_CLIENTS entries.




Note


Only used when client authentication is enabled



Accessed from crypto.c for client authentication




Definition at line 524 of file server/main.c.


524 {0};






Referenced by server_crypto_handshake(), and
    server_main().






h265_server_context_t* volatile g_h265_server = NULL


Global H.265 codec server context. Shared H.265 decoding context
    used by all packet handlers to decode client H.265 frames. Each client gets
    its own decoder instance.




THREAD SAFETY: The h265_server context is thread-safe and can be
    used concurrently by multiple client handlers. Decoders are per-client.
    During shutdown, set to NULL before destroying to prevent
  use-after-free.


Definition at line 186 of file server/main.c.


Referenced by handle_image_frame_h265_packet(), and
    server_main().






size_t
  g_num_server_identity_keys = 0


Number of loaded server identity keys. Number of server identity
    keys in use.




Tracks how many identity keys were successfully loaded from --key
    flags. Zero means server is running in simple mode (no identity key).


Definition at line 511 of file server/main.c.


Referenced by server_crypto_handshake().






size_t
  g_num_whitelisted_clients = 0


Number of whitelisted clients. Tracks the current number of
    entries in g_client_whitelist that are valid and active. Used to iterate the
    whitelist and check authorization.


Definition at line 534 of file server/main.c.


Referenced by server_crypto_handshake(), and
    server_main().






rate_limiter_t* g_rate_limiter = NULL


Global rate limiter for connection attempts and packet processing.
    Global connection rate limiter.




In-memory rate limiter to prevent connection flooding and DoS
    attacks. Tracks connection attempts and packet rates per IP address with
    configurable limits.




Default limits (from rate_limit.c):


    
  
  • RATE_EVENT_CONNECTION: 50 connections per 60 seconds
    • 
  
  • RATE_EVENT_IMAGE_FRAME: 144 FPS (8640 frames/min)
    • 
  
  • RATE_EVENT_AUDIO: 172 FPS (10320 packets/min)
    • 
  
  • RATE_EVENT_PING: 2 Hz (120 pings/min)
    • 
  
  • RATE_EVENT_CLIENT_JOIN: 10 joins per 60 seconds
    • 
  
  • RATE_EVENT_CONTROL: 100 control packets per 60 seconds
    • 



THREAD SAFETY: The rate limiter is thread-safe and can be used
    concurrently from the main accept loop and packet handlers without external
    synchronization.




See also


main.h for extern declaration


Definition at line 216 of file server/main.c.


Referenced by process_decrypted_packet(), and
    server_main().






bool g_server_encryption_enabled = false


Global flag indicating if server encryption is enabled. Server
    encryption enabled flag.




Set to true when the server is configured to use encryption and
    has successfully loaded a private key. Controls whether the server performs
    cryptographic handshakes with clients.




Note


Accessed from crypto.c for server-side crypto
  operations


Definition at line 475 of file server/main.c.


Referenced by server_crypto_handshake(), and

private_key_t
  g_server_identity_keys[MAX_IDENTITY_KEYS] = {0}


Global server identity keys array (multi-key support). Array of
    server identity keys.




Stores all server identity keys loaded from multiple --key flags.
    Enables servers to present different keys (SSH, GPG) based on client
    expectations. Server selects the appropriate key during handshake based on
    what the client downloaded from ACDS.




Note


g_server_private_key points to g_server_identity_keys[0]
  for compatibility


Definition at line 501 of file server/main.c.


501 {0};






Referenced by server_crypto_handshake().






private_key_t g_server_private_key = {0}


Global server private key (first identity key, for backward
    compatibility). Server's private key.




Stores the server's primary private key loaded from the first
    --key flag. Used for cryptographic handshakes and packet
    encryption/decryption. Initialized during server startup from the configured
    key file path.




Note


This is an alias to g_server_identity_keys[0] for
  backward compatibility



Accessed from crypto.c for server-side crypto operations

static_cond_t g_shutdown_cond =
  STATIC_COND_INIT


Global shutdown condition variable for waking blocked threads.
    Used to wake up threads that might be blocked on condition variables (like
    packet queues) during shutdown. This ensures responsive shutdown even when
    threads are waiting on blocking operations.
